title: Pilosella muscelii
intro: Pilosella muscicola, also known as Musk Ox's Beard, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella muscicola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its rarity and endangered status limit its availability and use in modern herbalism.
taxonomy: Pilosella muscelii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Pilosella is a group of plants that are often referred to as false dandelions due to their similar appearance. Within the Asteraceae family, Pilosella muscelii belongs to the tribe Cichorieae. The classification of Pilosella muscelii is further broken down into the binomial nomenclature, comprising the genus name Pilosella and the species name muscelii. This binomial system is used universally to identify and classify different species of plants.
